title: [CI:DOCS] Bump to v3.4.6
body: 3.4.5 wasn't rebased fully so we missed a minor security patch. Cut another release to fix.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.4/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@Luap99 I never remember that for release PRs. Oops.
@mheon i think [CI:DOCS] should be in the commit itself and not just PR title
Does that work? I thought we only scanned PR titles
Not sure, the tests still seem to be running FWIW.
Yeah, it requires a force-push after changing the name to retrigger CI and recognize the [CI:DOCS]. I'll change something in the readme.
@mheon checking about commit ordering, should the readme version change be before the 3.4.6 version change commit? So, maybe the 2nd commit and not the 4th commit?
Shouldn't matter, I just added it to kick CI
